summary: Small fighter common matching fix for `ftCo_80097D88` in `ftCo_DownBound.c`. The previous function body was factored into a local `static void inlineA1(Fighter_GObj* gobj)` helper, the fighter pointer load was changed from direct `gobj->user_data` to `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)`, and `ftCo_80097D88` became a thin wrapper calling `inlineA1(gobj)`. decomp.dev reported this produced one new GALE01 match: `ftCo_80097D88` improved from 0.00% to 100.00%, +56 bytes.
what_changed: Renamed/factored the old `ftCo_80097D88(Fighter_GObj* gobj)` body into `static void inlineA1(Fighter_GObj* gobj)`.
Changed the local fighter pointer initialization inside the factored body from `Fighter* fp = gobj->user_data;` to `Fighter* fp = GET_FIGHTER(gobj);`.
Added a new `void ftCo_80097D88(Fighter_GObj* gobj)` wrapper whose only statement is `inlineA1(gobj);`.
Preserved the behavioral branch: if `fp->x2228_b2` is set, call `ftCo_80097AF4(gobj)`; otherwise call `ftCo_8009794C(gobj)`.
decomp.dev reported one new match for `main/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DownBound` item `ftCo_80097D88`, +56 bytes, 0.00% before to 100.00% after.
smart_moves: Kept the known `ftCo_80097D88` function signature intact while using a local static helper to influence compiler output without changing the apparent game logic.
Used the project accessor macro `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` instead of raw `gobj->user_data`, a small source-level difference that mattered for matching-sensitive codegen.
Made the matching fix narrowly: one C file, unchanged branch targets, and a bot-verifiable +56 byte match.
decomp_lessons: When a tiny fighter function will not match as a direct body, try factoring the body into a `static` `inlineA#` helper and making the target symbol a wrapper; this can change inlining or call-shape enough to match.
Do not treat `gobj->user_data` and `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` as always interchangeable for decomp purposes; even equivalent-looking access patterns can affect generated code.
For PRs this small, the decomp.dev per-item report is strong evidence of success: here it specifically identifies `ftCo_80097D88` as newly 100% matched.
naming_conventions: `inlineA1` was used as a local static helper name for factored inline/matching logic.
The known address-style symbol name `ftCo_80097D88` was preserved for the matched function.
`GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` is the accessor idiom used in the final matching code for obtaining `Fighter*` from `Fighter_GObj*`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Static helper plus wrapper refactor to alter compiler code generation while preserving source-level behavior.
Accessor macro substitution (`GET_FIGHTER`) instead of direct struct member access (`gobj->user_data`) to obtain matching codegen.
Validated by decomp.dev GALE01 report: `ftCo_80097D88` moved from 0.00% to 100.00% matched, +56 bytes.
review_feedback: No human review comments or formal reviews were present in the slice.
The only feedback captured was the decomp.dev bot report confirming 1 new match for `ftCo_80097D88`.
follow_up_queries: Search for other `inlineA1` or `inlineA#` helper refactors used to match small fighter functions.
Search fighter code for direct `gobj->user_data` loads in nonmatching functions where `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` may affect codegen.
Look for other `ftCo_DownBound.c` functions near `ftCo_80097D40` and `ftCo_80097D88` that share the same `x2228_b2` branch logic.
Compare future or adjacent PRs touching `ftCo_DownBound.c` to see whether this helper pattern was reused or renamed.
